state_description: Mixed fleet of electric urban trains and diesel-powered regional trains representing the national average. Urban metro/suburban systems (Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth, Adelaide) are predominantly electric; regional and interstate services are diesel. National average ~50% electric, 50% diesel by energy content. Electricity efficiency improves and diesel share declines as electrification progresses. AES 2025 Table F1 rail (2023-24): passenger share 16 PJ / 22,000 million_pkm = 727 GJ/million_pkm. Split: 586 GJ electricity (urban metro), 141 GJ diesel (regional).
cost_components_summary: Annualised non-fuel operating cost per million passenger-kilometres (real 2024 AUD), excluding explicit energy commodity purchases and carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: AES 2025 Table F1 rail (2023-24): passenger share 16 PJ / 22,000 million_pkm = 727 GJ/million_pkm. Split by energy: electricity 586 (urban metro), diesel 141 GJ/million_pkm (2025). 2050: electricity 627 (further urban rail growth), diesel 95 (fewer diesel services).
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct scope 1 diesel combustion only. Electricity upstream (scope 2) excluded; captured by the electricity supply family. Green hydrogen combustion CO2 = 0. No process emissions for rail passenger services.
rollout_limit_notes: National adoption bound reflecting rail fleet replacement cycles (~30-40 yr), infrastructure investment lead times, and state government network investment programmes.
evidence_summary: AES 2025 Table F1 rail (passenger share); BITRE Rail Summary Data 2023-24; NGGI rail.
derivation_method: Energy coefficients from AES 2025 Table F1 rail (64.9 PJ total = 52 PJ diesel + 12.9 PJ electricity, 2023-24); passenger share = 16.0 PJ (electricity 12.9 + regional diesel 3.1). BITRE 22,000 million_pkm gives 727.0 GJ/million_pkm. Split: 586 GJ electricity (urban metro) + 141 GJ diesel (regional). NGGI check (scope 1 diesel): 9.9 × 22,000 / 1e6 = 0.22 MtCO2e.
review_notes: 50/50 energy split is a national average approximation. State-level electric shares vary significantly (NSW high electric; QLD/WA high diesel regional). Phase 2: disaggregate by state and service type.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Disaggregate into urban electric, suburban electric, regional diesel, and intercity diesel sub-families.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Maps to national passenger rail demand technology.

state_description: Full overhead line (OHL) electrification of urban, suburban and intercity rail corridors. Eliminates diesel for electrified routes. Energy intensity 250 GJ/million_pkm (0.25 MJ/pkm; consistent with loaded urban trains) improving to 200 GJ/million_pkm by 2050 via regenerative braking and lighter rolling stock. Zero scope 1 CO2e; electricity scope 2 excluded per A003.
cost_components_summary: Annualised non-fuel operating cost per million passenger-kilometres (real 2024 AUD), excluding explicit energy commodity purchases and carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: Electric rail energy intensity: 250 GJ electricity/million_pkm (2025) = 0.25 MJ/pkm. Improving to 200 GJ/million_pkm (2050) with regenerative braking retrofits and new-generation rolling stock. Scope 1 CO2e = 0.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct scope 1 diesel combustion only. Electricity upstream (scope 2) excluded; captured by the electricity supply family. Green hydrogen combustion CO2 = 0. No process emissions for rail passenger services.
rollout_limit_notes: National adoption bound reflecting rail fleet replacement cycles (~30-40 yr), infrastructure investment lead times, and state government network investment programmes.
evidence_summary: NSW TrainLink electric fleet energy data; Melbourne Metro operational statistics; BITRE rail energy intensity estimates; UIC Railway Statistics Summary.
derivation_method: Energy coefficients from AES 2025 Table F1 rail (64.9 PJ total = 52 PJ diesel + 12.9 PJ electricity, 2023-24); passenger share = 16.0 PJ (electricity 12.9 + regional diesel 3.1). BITRE 22,000 million_pkm gives 727.0 GJ/million_pkm. Split: 586 GJ electricity (urban metro) + 141 GJ diesel (regional). NGGI check (scope 1 diesel): 9.9 × 22,000 / 1e6 = 0.22 MtCO2e.
review_notes: OHL electrification is capital-intensive and requires long planning/construction lead times. Max share of 60% reflects the electrifiable network fraction. Some regional routes may remain non-electrified.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Add explicit OHL infrastructure investment and electrification programme schedule.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Maps to electrified rail passenger demand node.

state_description: Hydrogen fuel cell multiple unit (HFCMU) trains for non-electrified regional routes, replacing diesel. Fuel cell efficiency advantage partially offset by hydrogen production and storage losses. Energy intensity 320 GJ H2/million_pkm (2030) improving to 250 GJ H2/million_pkm (2050). Zero scope 1 CO2e with green hydrogen supply. Available from 2030 (not commercially deployed in Australia before 2030).
cost_components_summary: Annualised non-fuel operating cost per million passenger-kilometres (real 2024 AUD), excluding explicit energy commodity purchases and carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: H2 FC train: diesel energy 182 GJ/million_pkm equivalent → H2 FC efficiency advantage gives ~300-320 GJ H2/million_pkm (2030) accounting for H2 chain losses. Improving to 250 GJ H2/million_pkm (2050). Scope 1 CO2e = 0. Not available 2025.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct scope 1 diesel combustion only. Electricity upstream (scope 2) excluded; captured by the electricity supply family. Green hydrogen combustion CO2 = 0. No process emissions for rail passenger services.
rollout_limit_notes: National adoption bound reflecting rail fleet replacement cycles (~30-40 yr), infrastructure investment lead times, and state government network investment programmes.
availability_conditions: Available from 2030; zero coefficients and max_share in 2025.
evidence_summary: Alstom Coradia iLint performance data; Siemens Mireo Plus H fuel cell train; Queensland hydrogen train trial; ARENA hydrogen rail roadmap 2022.
derivation_method: Energy coefficients from AES 2025 Table F1 rail (64.9 PJ total = 52 PJ diesel + 12.9 PJ electricity, 2023-24); passenger share = 16.0 PJ (electricity 12.9 + regional diesel 3.1). BITRE 22,000 million_pkm gives 727.0 GJ/million_pkm. Split: 586 GJ electricity (urban metro) + 141 GJ diesel (regional). NGGI check (scope 1 diesel): 9.9 × 22,000 / 1e6 = 0.22 MtCO2e.
review_notes: H2 rail is at demonstration scale in Australia. Commercial deployment expected from 2030-2035. Green H2 supply chain maturity is the binding constraint.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Add explicit H2 refuelling station infrastructure; distinguish on-board H2 storage options.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Maps to hydrogen regional rail demand node.

candidate_expansion_pathway: Add explicit H2 refuelling station infrastructure; distinguish on-board H2 storage options.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Maps to hydrogen regional rail demand node.

state_description: Hydrogen fuel cell multiple unit (HFCMU) trains for non-electrified regional routes, replacing diesel. state_description: Battery-electric multiple unit trains for short-to-medium non-electrified regional routes (50-200 km). Charged at electrified termini or charging stations. Slightly higher energy intensity than OHL trains due to battery weight and charging overhead (280 GJ/million_pkm → 230 GJ/million_pkm). Limited range; not suitable for long regional routes. Zero scope 1 CO2e.
cost_components_summary: Annualised non-fuel operating cost per million passenger-kilometres (real 2024 AUD), excluding explicit energy commodity purchases and carbon costs.
input_basis_notes: Battery-electric train: 280 GJ electricity/million_pkm (2025; battery weight penalty ~10-15% vs OHL), improving to 230 GJ/million_pkm (2050). Scope 1 CO2e = 0; electricity scope 2 excluded per A003.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct scope 1 diesel combustion only. Electricity upstream (scope 2) excluded; captured by the electricity supply family. Green hydrogen combustion CO2 = 0. No process emissions for rail passenger services.
rollout_limit_notes: National adoption bound reflecting rail fleet replacement cycles (~30-40 yr), infrastructure investment lead times, and state government network investment programmes.
evidence_summary: Hitachi AT300 battery train performance; Bombardier Talent 3 battery data; ARENA battery rail feasibility study; European battery train deployments.
derivation_method: Energy coefficients from AES 2025 Table F1 rail (64.9 PJ total = 52 PJ diesel + 12.9 PJ electricity, 2023-24); passenger share = 16.0 PJ (electricity 12.9 + regional diesel 3.1). BITRE 22,000 million_pkm gives 727.0 GJ/million_pkm. Split: 586 GJ electricity (urban metro) + 141 GJ diesel (regional). NGGI check (scope 1 diesel): 9.9 × 22,000 / 1e6 = 0.22 MtCO2e.
review_notes: Battery range (typically <150 km per charge) limits applicability. Max share 15% reflects short regional route fraction. Competes with hydrogen on medium regional routes.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Add explicit range and charging constraint by route class.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: Maps to battery-electric regional rail demand node.
